      • all(c: VectorCollection): (string, seq[float32])

A simple vector store implementation with optional disk persistence and write-ahead logging (WAL) for durability. The vector store supports multiple named collections, each with a specified dimension for the vectors.

Vectors are stored in-memory in an ordered table for fast lookups, and can be persisted to disk as a snapshot. The WAL allows for durability and crash recovery by logging all changes to the vector store before they are applied, enabling the store to be reconstructed after a crash by replaying the WAL entries.

Imports

../wal

Types

DistanceMetric = enum
  dmCosine, dmDot, dmL2
Defines the distance metric to use for nearest neighbor search. This allows the nearest procedure to compute similarity or distance between vectors in different ways, depending on the use case
  • dmCosine: cosine similarity, where higher is more similar (range -1 to 1)
  • dmDot: dot product, where higher is more similar (unbounded)
  • dmL2: L2 distance (squared), where lower is more similar (range 0 to inf)
VectorCollection {.acyclic.} = ref object
  name*: string
  dimension*: int
Represents a collection of vectors, identified by a name and a fixed dimension. Each vector is associated with a primary key (pk) for lookup. 
VectorStorageMode = enum
  smInMemory, smDisk
Defines the storage mode for the vector store, which can be either in-memory or disk-based. 
VectorStore {.acyclic.} = ref object
The main data structure for the vector store, containing multiple collections of vectors 
VectorStoreError = object of CatchableError
Custom exception type for errors related to the vector store operations, such as invalid input, collection not found, or WAL issues.

Procs

proc checkpoint(s: VectorStore) {....raises: [IOError, WalError, OSError, Exception], tags: [
    WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}
Manually triggers a checkpoint by flushing the WAL and saving a snapshot to disk, ensuring that all committed operations are persisted and the WAL is truncated up to the checkpoint LSN. This can be used to reduce recovery time after a crash by minimizing the number of WAL entries that need to be replayed. 
proc createCollection(s: VectorStore; c: VectorCollection) {.
    ...raises: [IOError, WalError, VectorStoreError, OSError, Exception], tags: [
    TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Creates a new collection in the vector store with the specified name and dimension, logging the operation in the WAL if enabled 
proc delete(s: VectorStore; collection, pk: string): bool {.
    ...raises: [IOError, WalError, KeyError, OSError, Exception], tags: [
    TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Delete a vector from the specified collection by primary key (pk). The operation is logged in the WAL if enabled. Returns true if successfully deleted, false if the pk was not found. 
proc dropCollection(s: VectorStore; name: string) {.
    ...raises: [IOError, WalError, VectorStoreError, OSError, Exception], tags: [
    TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Drops the specified collection from the vector store, logging the operation in the WAL if enabled 
proc get(c: VectorCollection; pk: string): Option[seq[float32]] {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc get(s: VectorStore; collection, pk: string): Option[seq[float32]] {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Retrieves a vector from the specified collection by primary key (pk). Returns an option containing the vector if found, or none if not found or if the collection does not exist. 
proc getCollection(s: VectorStore; name: string): Option[VectorCollection] {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hasCollection(s: VectorStore; name: string): bool {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
proc insert(s: VectorStore; collection, pk: string; vec: seq[float32]) {....raises: [
    VectorStoreError, KeyError, IOError, WalError, OSError, Exception], tags: [
    TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Inserts a vector into the specified collection with the given primary key (pk). The vector is validated against the collection's dimension, and the operation is logged in the WAL if enabled 
proc nearest(c: VectorCollection; query: seq[float32]; k: int;
             metric: DistanceMetric = dmCosine): seq[(string, float32)] {.
    ...raises: [VectorStoreError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Finds the k nearest vectors to the query in the collection using the given distance metric. The result is a sequence of tuples containing the primary key and the corresponding similarity score or distance, sorted by relevance according to the specified metric. 
proc nearest(s: VectorStore; collection: string; query: seq[float32]; k: int;
             metric: DistanceMetric = dmCosine): seq[(string, float32)] {.
    ...raises: [VectorStoreError, KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Finds the k nearest vectors to the query in the specified collection using the given distance metric 
proc newCollection(name: string; dimension: int): VectorCollection {.
    ...raises: [VectorStoreError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc newInMemoryVectorStore(): VectorStore {....raises: [VectorStoreError, IOError,
    WalError, OSError, JsonParsingError, ValueError, KeyError, Exception],
    tags: [ReadDirEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, WriteDirEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Convenience procedure to create a new in-memory vector store without persistence or WAL, useful for testing or ephemeral use cases 
proc newVectorStore(path: string; mode: VectorStorageMode = smDisk;
                    enableWal: bool = true; checkpointEveryOps: uint32 = 0'u32;
                    walFlushEveryOps: uint32 = 1000'u32): VectorStore {....raises: [
    VectorStoreError, IOError, WalError, OSError, JsonParsingError, ValueError,
    KeyError, Exception],
    tags: [ReadDirEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, WriteDirEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Creates a new VectorStore instance with the specified storage mode, WAL settings, and checkpointing configuration. 
proc recoverFromWal(s: VectorStore) {....raises: [IOError, VectorStoreError,
    WalError, OSError, JsonParsingError, ValueError, KeyError, Exception], tags: [
    ReadDirEffect, ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect, WriteDirEffect], forbids: [].}
